reattempts.title: First-tool latency / discoverability impact of ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=true

new_attempts.result: Phase 1 has only one external file. A deeper reread added sharper discoverability evidence and clarified that only six primary tools stay upfront, but no latency benchmark exists.

new_evidence.quote: The post says most users would not know the setting exists and that only six primary tools stay loaded while the rest lazy-load.
The author calls ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH plus context hygiene the highest-leverage configuration win.

reattempts.iter_10_answer: Iter-10 strengthens the discoverability side: the source explicitly says most users would not know the setting exists, and it explains the mechanism as keeping six primary tools upfront while lazy-loading the rest. What still does not exist is any first-tool latency, fallback, or task-success benchmark.
reattempts.iter_10_residual: No measured first-tool latency, lazy-load penalty, fallback rate, or task-completion delta appears anywhere in the packet's only external source.
reattempts.improvement_summary: Discoverability is now supported more directly, but the latency half of the gap remains unmeasured.

reattempts.title: Edit-retry root-cause attribution across prompt | workflow | guardrail buckets

new_attempts.result: No new external files exist in phase 1. The deeper reread again found only the aggregate count of 31 edit-retry chains, with no per-chain examples or cause labels.

new_evidence.quote: The post only says there were 31 failed-edit-then-retry sequences.

reattempts.iter_10_answer: UNKNOWN-confirmed. Iter-10 found no new per-chain evidence, no examples, and no author-supplied cause labels. The source still supports only the existence of 31 edit-retry chains, not attribution into prompt, workflow, or guardrail buckets.
reattempts.iter_10_residual: Closure requires chain-level transcripts, error text, or a source-authored classification. None are present in the packet's sole external artifact.
reattempts.improvement_summary: Iter-9's semantic caution holds: this is an evidence-label gap, not a measurement gap.

reattempts.title: Net-cost audit of /clear + plugin-memory remedy bundle vs stale-resume baseline

new_attempts.result: The deeper reread exposed the exact compensating surfaces behind /clear: startup injection, session lineage tracking, recall search, and the auditor skill. The post still never subtracts their cost.

new_evidence.quote: The post contrasts /compact, stale resume, and /clear, then describes the warning hooks around expiry and resume.
The /clear remedy bundle includes startup context injection, prior-session lineage, recall search, and the auditor skill.

reattempts.iter_10_answer: Iter-10 narrows the bundle mechanics, not the accounting. We can now name the overhead surfaces the stale-resume comparison omitted, but the source still never prices injected startup context, recall lookups, hook writes, or auditor runs against the resume baseline.
reattempts.iter_10_residual: No per-session token totals exist for startup injection, recall invocations, hook prompts, or auditor-trigger overhead, so the bundle remains gross-savings-only.
reattempts.improvement_summary: The missing net-cost terms are more concrete now, but still not measurable from the available source.

reattempts.title: Reddit-post arithmetic reconciliation

new_attempts.result: Iter-10 traced the arithmetic line-by-line. Some sub-math is internally consistent, but the source still never reconciles the 926-session headline with the 858-session body dataset.

new_evidence.quote: The title sentence says the audit covered 926 sessions.
The 264M estimate is computed from 14,000 saved per turn, 22 turns per session, and 858 sessions.
The body reports 858 sessions, 18,903 turns, and 6,152 out of 11,357 idle-gap turns.

reattempts.iter_10_answer: Iter-10 confirms partial reconciliation only. The 264M headline is locally reproducible from the source's own 14,000 x 22 x 858 arithmetic, and the 54% idle-gap ratio matches 6,152/11,357. But the post still does not explain why the headline says 926 sessions or why the idle-gap denominator is 11,357 instead of 18,903.
reattempts.iter_10_residual: The remaining inconsistencies are source-bounded and require author clarification or the underlying SQLite/JSONL dataset.
reattempts.improvement_summary: The self-consistent sub-calculations are clearer, but the top-line dataset mismatch remains unresolved.

reattempts.title: Monorepo support beyond pnpm + npm workspaces

new_attempts.result: The new files show exactly what monorepo support means in practice: one pnpm fixture, one package.json workspace test, and README wording that generalizes package.json workspaces to npm/yarn.

new_evidence.quote: Monorepo detection is keyed off package.json workspaces or pnpm-workspace.yaml.
Workspace patterns come only from pnpm-workspace.yaml or package.json workspaces arrays.
The only direct monorepo test uses package.json workspaces and checks cross-workspace aggregation.
The only eval monorepo fixture uses pnpm-workspace.yaml.
README scopes monorepo support to pnpm, npm, and yarn workspaces.

reattempts.iter_10_answer: Closed. Iter-10 makes the real support boundary explicit: codesight supports monorepos only through standard workspace manifests, namely pnpm-workspace.yaml and package.json workspaces. That covers pnpm and generic npm/yarn workspaces, but not nx/turbo/lerna/rush metadata unless those repos also mirror membership into one of those manifests.
reattempts.iter_10_residual: The exact silent-miss rate in exotic monorepos is still unmeasured, but the source-level support boundary is now clear enough to close the scope question.
reattempts.improvement_summary: The gap is now bounded by concrete manifest support instead of a vague 'beyond pnpm/npm' warning.

reattempts.title: Blast-radius BFS leak and heuristic schema-impact false-positive rate

new_attempts.result: The deeper pass shows the evaluation docs over-promise blast-radius coverage, but the fixtures and eval implementation never encode or score blast radius at all.

new_evidence.quote: Dependents are enqueued before the depth guard cuts traversal, which creates the one-hop leak risk.
Any affected file with a db-tagged route can mark every schema model as affected.
The eval README says ground truth covers routes, models, env vars, and blast radius.
GroundTruth only defines routes, models, components, envVars, and middleware.
The eval harness runs routes, schema, components, config, and middleware detectors only; blast radius is never scored.

reattempts.iter_10_answer: Iter-10 strengthens the unmeasured-rate conclusion. The BFS leak and schema over-fire mechanisms are source-confirmed, and the deeper eval pass shows there is still no blast-radius benchmark despite README wording that implies one. So the qualitative risk is closed, but the false-positive rate remains unquantified.
reattempts.iter_10_residual: No fixture, ground truth, or eval metric measures blast-radius precision/recall, so frequency and severity of false positives remain unknown.
reattempts.improvement_summary: The repo now shows not just that the rate is missing, but that the evaluation lane never implemented blast-radius scoring at all.

reattempts.title: 93% token reduction is estimated from fixed constants, not benchmarked against Public's typed stack

new_attempts.result: The new files show the synthetic savings math is not just in implementation; it is codified in tests and repeated in generated agent guidance as a canned 25k-vs-500 story.

new_evidence.quote: The stats test expects saved tokens from 7 local queries and 3 cache hits using fixed 25,000-token savings constants.
Generated OpenClaw skill text advertises 'Saves 93% of tokens.'
The skill tells agents the context tool costs ~500 tokens versus ~25,000 for manual exploration.

reattempts.iter_10_answer: Closed. Iter-10 confirms the 93% claim is a productized estimate, not a benchmark: the 25k-per-query savings model is asserted in tests and propagated into generated agent instructions as a canned 500-vs-25k story. There is still no side-by-side benchmark against Public's typed CocoIndex+CodeGraph+Memory stack.
reattempts.iter_10_residual: A future benchmark could still compare Contextador against Public, but the honesty question for the current 93% claim is now decisively answered.
reattempts.improvement_summary: The savings claim is now traced through code, tests, and generated prompt surfaces, which closes the 'estimated vs benchmarked' question.

reattempts.title: 71.5x token reduction is heuristic math, not Anthropic count_tokens-grounded

new_attempts.result: The benchmark source and tests show the headline ratio is entirely built from approximations and internal consistency checks, with no provider token API anywhere in the measurement path.

new_evidence.quote: Benchmark tokenization is based on a fixed 4 chars-per-token approximation.
Corpus tokens are derived from graph size or supplied word counts, then divided by estimated query tokens to produce the reduction ratio.
The tests only check that reduction_ratio exists and that corpus_tokens scale linearly with the corpus_words heuristic.

reattempts.iter_10_answer: Closed. Iter-10 confirms the 71.5x figure is heuristic math end-to-end: approximate chars-per-token tokenization, approximate corpus-word conversion, and tests that validate only proportionality and presence of a ratio. There is no Anthropic count_tokens or comparable provider-grounded token measurement in the benchmark path.
reattempts.iter_10_residual: A count_tokens rerun would still be valuable, but the current benchmark's evidence quality is no longer ambiguous.
reattempts.improvement_summary: The repo's own tests now make it clear the benchmark is validating heuristics, not model-counted token usage.

reattempts.title: mixed-corpus multimodal claim is not supported by the checked-in worked artifact

new_attempts.result: The new files explain the mismatch: the repo does not ship the PNG used in the live multimodal review, so the checked-in worked artifact is inherently not a self-contained multimodal proof.

new_evidence.quote: The README says the original benchmark included attention_arabic.png, but PNG files are not stored in the repo and must be added manually to reproduce.
The README says the checked-in corpus has 4 files and that only if the image is included should you expect one extra vision-derived node.
The checked-in report is a 4-file corpus with 0 input and 0 output tokens.
The live evaluation review, by contrast, describes a separate 5-file run that included an Arabic PNG image.

reattempts.iter_10_answer: Closed. The checked-in mixed-corpus artifact is not a self-contained multimodal proof. The repo's own README says the benchmark image is omitted from version control and must be added manually, while the checked-in GRAPH_REPORT shows a 4-file, 0-token artifact. The multimodal narrative in review.md refers to a separate live run, not the shipped worked folder as-is.
reattempts.iter_10_residual: What remains open is only whether a freshly reproduced run with the missing PNG matches the live review; the artifact-mismatch question itself is closed.
reattempts.improvement_summary: Iter-10 turns a suspected packaging drift into a precise explanation: the multimodal evidence lives in a live review, not in the checked-in worked artifact.

reattempts.title: PreToolUse Glob|Grep nudge effectiveness is still unmeasured

new_attempts.result: The new files show that the repo tests installation, idempotence, and removal of the hook, but there is still no telemetry or before/after behavior measurement.

new_evidence.quote: The hook literally just echoes guidance before Glob or Grep when graphify-out/graph.json exists.
Install logic only appends the PreToolUse hook into settings.json.
Tests verify creation, idempotence, and uninstall of the settings hook, not its behavioral effect.

reattempts.iter_10_answer: UNKNOWN-confirmed. Iter-10 closes the implementation question but not the effectiveness question: the repo definitely installs a surgical echo-only PreToolUse hook, and it tests registration hygiene, but it still contains no telemetry, experiment, or before/after usage data showing that Glob/Grep behavior actually changes.
reattempts.iter_10_residual: A real closure needs tool-usage telemetry or comparative task runs with the hook enabled versus disabled.
reattempts.improvement_summary: The hook's exact mechanism is now clearer, which confirms the remaining unknown is behavioral evidence only.
